title: When You Are Not Sure God Is There
scripture_text: My tears have been my food day and night, while people say to me continually, "Where is your God?"
reflection: Doubt is not the opposite of faith. Indifference is. As long as you are asking the question, you are still in the conversation.
body:
It is okay to not be sure. The Bible is full of people who were not sure. David cried out, "How long, O Lord?" Job demanded an audience. Thomas needed to see the wounds. They made it into the story, and so will you.

Doubt is often faith's honest form. It is what faith looks like when it stops pretending.

You do not have to perform certainty. You do not have to defend God. He can handle your questions. He has been handling them since Abraham, since Moses, since the Israelites in the desert who wondered, every forty years, why they were following this cloud.

Be honest. Be faithful to the honesty. The rest will follow.
prayer: Lord, I believe; help my unbelief. That is all I have right now. It is enough. Amen.
